text.title: GCP Network Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the gcp-network-architecture methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Use Shared VPC for any environment with more than one GCP project; the VPC lives in a dedicated host project.
rule.rationale: Shared VPC centralises subnet + firewall control with the network team while letting service projects deploy workloads.

rule.statement: Allocate /16 per environment VPC; within each VPC reserve /20-/24 for primary subnets, /18-/20 for GKE pod ranges, /24 for GKE services, /28 for GKE master CIDR.
rule.rationale: A documented plan prevents the future GKE-pod-range collisions that block adding new clusters.

rule.statement: Use Private Service Connect for projects without public IPs that call Google APIs; add private DNS zones for googleapis.com and gcr.io.
rule.rationale: PSC plus private DNS lets workloads reach Google without an internet egress path.

rule.statement: Configure Cloud NAT per region with explicit min-ports-per-VM and dynamic-port-allocation; alert on NAT-port exhaustion.
rule.rationale: NAT-port exhaustion looks like silent connection failures; explicit sizing + alert detects it before user impact.

rule.statement: Apply hierarchical firewall policies at folder level with explicit deny-all egress and per-tier allow rules.
rule.rationale: Folder-level policies cannot be over-ridden by a project-level mistake — the only durable network guard rail.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
